summary: Content snapshot/rollback system tested end-to-end on demo-couk (backend + frontend). All 12 new snapshot tests pass; all 56 existing regression tests still pass. UI Restore points modal opens, shows colour badges, saves manual snapshots and restores are undoable.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py update_region (line 417-424) is MISSING scope_ok() check while update_link/page_op/create_page/delete_page all enforce it — an editor scoped to site A could edit text regions on site B. Low practical impact (must know eids) but a real RBAC hole; add: `if not scope_ok(u, slug_site): raise HTTPException(403,...)`.
update_seo (line 478-482) is likewise missing scope_ok() check.
make_snapshot signature `body: dict = {}` uses a mutable default — swap to `body: dict | None = Body(default=None)` for safety.

restore_snapshot deletes pages then re-inserts non-atomically; a mid-restore crash would leave the site empty. Consider inserting into a temp collection or wrapping in a transaction (if replicaset available).
seed_data_creation: Created editor user scoped to demo-couk for RBAC test (kept in DB — main agent can delete via /api/users if desired). Backend test_99 re-ingested both demo-couk and wifetobe at the end to restore pristine content. Snapshots collection contains multiple test-generated pre-publish/manual/auto rows for demo-couk from the run — safe to leave (baseline import still present).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_snapshots.py covers: import baseline uniqueness, re-ingest idempotency, manual snapshot, auto-snapshot throttle (3 rapid edits ≤1 snapshot), pre-publish snapshot, rollback reverts text edits AND creates 'Before restore', rollback is undoable via that Before restore, structural rollback (page count + add-image), editor RBAC scope (demo-couk allowed / wifetobe 403), unauth 401. Run with `pytest backend/tests/test_snapshots.py -v`.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in the snapshot/rollback flow itself. Only pre-existing missing scope_ok checks on update_region/update_seo flagged as code-review items (not part of this feature).
